description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

The chairperson of the Hanno City Food Life Improvement Promotion Council, Keiko Kojima, received the 2025 (Reiwa 7) Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare Commendation for Nutrition-Related Achievement and a Certificate of Appreciation for Food Life Improvement Achievement at the Saitama Prefecture Health Promotion Gathering. Since joining in 1986 (Showa 61), Ms. Kojima has engaged in lifestyle improvement awareness activities for over 40 years and has served as North Branch Director and Chairperson since 2014 (Heisei 26).

During the courtesy visit, she spoke about how public awareness of "salt reduction" is increasing and salt intake is declining, yet further promotion is still needed. She emphasized the importance of families becoming familiar with salt reduction at home.

Food Life Improvement Promotion volunteers work under the slogan "Our health is in our hands," conducting health promotion activities through food. Across five branches in Hanno (North, East, Kaji East, Kaji West, and Haraichiba), they hold cooking workshops about five times per year, parent-child cooking classes, and food education activities at city events.

In fiscal year 2026 (Reiwa 8), a new Promotion Volunteer Training Course will be held. Participants will learn correct knowledge about nutrition and health from registered dietitians and health nurses, and upon completion will be registered as Food Life Improvement Promotion volunteers. Details will be announced later. New participants are being recruited to join the cause.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kenkohukusibu/hokencenter/8239.html
summary: ℹ️ Hanno City Food Life Improvement Promotion Council
